data.title: Key facts: LVMH shares rise 42%; luxury sector recovery in focus
data.description:
• LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton shares rose 42% in three months, indicating a recovery in the luxury sector, amid ongoing concerns about the upcoming holiday season's performance.1
  • LVMH and other fashion houses are facing pressure to show that the recovery observed in Q3 can translate into sustained growth during the upcoming holiday season.2

data.title: Key facts: Deutsche Bank named Stabilisation Coordinator; DWS to acquire 40% stake
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank AG was named Stabilisation Coordinator for a securities offer by Banque et Caisse d'Epargne de l'Etat, Luxembourg, to support market prices during the stabilisation period.1
  • Deutsche Bank's investment arm, DWS Group, is set to acquire a 40% stake in a unit of Nippon Life India Asset Management.2

data.title: CCI FRANCE MALAYSIA GALA NIGHT 2025: CELEBRATING FRANCE-MALAYSIA PARTNERSHIP AND STRENGTHENED BILATERAL RELATIONS
data.description:
strong

The Malaysian French Chamber of Commerce & Industry / CCI France Malaysia (CCIFM) hosted its Gala Night on 13th November 2025 at The St. Regis Kuala Lumpur, celebrating the Malaysian-French business community and its vital contributions to the robust bilateral trade between France and Malaysia.

This year marked a new milestone in Malaysia-France relations, with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s official visit to Paris reaffirming the strong diplomatic and economic ties between both nations, paving the way for enhanced cooperation in trade, innovation, and sustainable development. Building on the momentum of this visit, a business delegation of 29 French companies, organised by Business France and MEDEF International, undertook a mission to Malaysia to further advance bilateral ties.

The French economic footprint in Malaysia continues to grow, with over 600 French entities operating locally, including approximately 300 subsidiaries and 300 entrepreneurs. These entities collectively provide employment to about 30,000 people and have contributed an accumulated turnover of RM20 billion to the Malaysian economy.

Over 400 guests were welcomed by Datuk Zainal Amanshah and Mr Richard Fostier, Honorary Chairman and President of CCIFM, respectively, alongside Mr. Olivier Sigaud, Deputy Head of Mission at the Embassy of France in Malaysia. The night was further graced by the presence of Guest of Honor, YB Senator Tengku Datuk Seri Utama Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz, Minister of Investment, Trade & Industry, Malaysia, of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI) Malaysia.

The key partners of the night were TotalEnergies, Air Liquide, Airbus, Arkema, BNP Paribas, Botanickel, Cegos, Club Med, Engie, L’Oréal, Safran, Saint Gobain, SeaOwl Group, STMicroelectronics, Technip Energies, CMA CGM, JLL, Michelin, Rotortrade, Société Générale, and Veolia.

With a long-standing history of dynamic trade relations, France and Malaysia continue to exemplify the strength and resilience of their economic partnership. The Gala Night served as a platform to reflect on this enduring relationship, celebrate shared achievements, and look ahead to new opportunities in bilateral trade. The Chamber is confident about Malaysia's standing as the undisputed strategic hub for French businesses in Southeast Asia and its future role as a central gateway to the region.

This success is built upon the strong collaboration between French and Malaysian institutions, notably the French Embassy and its Economic Department, Business France, French Trade Advisors, La French Tech, MIDA, MATRADE, InvestKL, and other state investment promotion agencies, Talent Corp, and MDEC with CCI France Malaysia.

Selected member companies were honoured with the CCIFM Excellence Award, recognising individuals, companies, and organisations that have demonstrated exceptional excellence and innovation in their respective fields. Their achievements continue to drive the success of Malaysian-French business relations, inspiring greater collaboration and prosperity for both nations.

The following companies were awarded:

· “Outstanding Achievements – Large Company” was granted to

strong

· “Outstanding Achievements – SME” was granted to

strong

· “Entrepreneur of the Year” was granted to

strong

· “Corporate Social Responsibility” was granted to

strong

· “France-Malaysia Smart Partnership” was granted to

strong

· “Image of France” was granted to

strong

The evening came alive with captivating performances by renowned artists, including Jaclyn Victor, Douglas Lim, Psycusix, and Charity Goh. The evening concluded with fortunate guests walking away with a four-night stay at Club Med Phuket, courtesy of Club Med, and an economy and a business class return ticket to Paris, from our airline partner Malaysia Airlines.

What began last year with our four-year tree-planting program has now grown to 500 trees, a testament to CCIFM’s unwavering commitment to sustainability and its long-term reforestation efforts in the Lower Kinabatangan Wildlife Sanctuary in Sabah, made possible through the continued support of the Malaysian-French Business Community. These trees help restore biodiversity for wildlife while also offsetting approximately 12 tonnes of CO₂ emissions each year.

See more photos:

a

The

a

is to foster a dynamic business ecosystem between its members and the Malaysian business landscape, facilitating meaningful connections and actively supporting the growth of trade and investment opportunities. Strategically located in the heart of Kuala Lumpur’s business and transportation hub, CCIFM offers

a

with fully equipped private offices and coworking spaces. Designed for French entrepreneurs, SMEs, and multinational companies expanding into Malaysia and the Asia-Pacific region, CCIFM provides business center services, company set-up and registration, immigration and visa assistance, accounting, payroll and commercial services such as organizing prospective missions, partner searches, and various market research initiatives.

data.title: Key facts: Volkswagen supply chain issues; investment approval delayed; Fitch to rate class A notes
data.description:
• Volkswagen is facing supply chain issues with Nexperia, halting silicon wafer shipments to China. The company is negotiating to buy wafers from Nexperia's Hamburg factory to ease chip shortages.1
  • Volkswagen's supervisory board has delayed approving an investment package due to worries about the company's finances, with no comments from the company on the issue.2
  • Fitch Ratings plans to assign ratings to Volkswagen's class A notes from the Auto Loan Enhanced Trust 2025-2, including a short-term F1+(EXP)sf and long-term AAA(EXP)sf.3

data.title: Key facts: TotalEnergies signs LNG deal; considers asset sale in Asia; partners on Oman wind farms
data.description:
• TotalEnergies secured a 20-year deal with Western LNG for 2 million tonnes of LNG annually from the Ksi Lisims project, pending final investment decision. They also bought a 5% stake in Western LNG.1
  • TotalEnergies is considering selling renewable energy assets in Asia, valued at hundreds of millions, to cut debt. The company is consulting an adviser to gauge buyer interest.2
  • TotalEnergies partners with OQ Alternative Energy on the Riyah 1 and 2 wind farms in Oman, featuring 36 turbines and a total capacity of 200 MW, boosting clean energy efforts.3

data.title: Key facts: Rheinmetall to raise sales guidance; key player in defense sector
data.description:
• Rheinmetall is anticipated to raise its midterm sales guidance next week, with analysts from Morgan Stanley and MWB-research expecting updates during the forthcoming capital markets day.1
  • Rheinmetall, valued at about 78 billion euros, plays a key role in Europe's military buildup due to increased defense spending following the Russian invasion of Ukraine.2

data.title: Key facts: Novo Nordisk won't raise Metsera bid; WeightWatchers plans Wegovy pill
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk will not raise its bid for Metsera after shareholders backed Pfizer's $10 billion offer, causing a 1.4% drop in its U.S. shares to $49.54.1
  • WeightWatchers will launch Novo Nordisk's Wegovy in pill form in 2026, aiming to boost its branded obesity medication lineup after recovering from bankruptcy.2
  • Metsera's MET-097i, a monthly GLP-1 receptor agonist, aims to rival Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, which is given weekly, showing strong weight loss results in trials.3

data.title: Key facts: Novartis' KLU156 trial achieves 97% cure rates; partners with BioArctic
data.description:
• Novartis' Phase III trial for malaria treatment KLU156 (GanLum) met its primary goal, achieving over 97% cure rates across 12 African countries, surpassing standard treatments.12
  • Novartis has partnered with BioArctic, paying USD 30 million upfront to develop a treatment combining BioArctic's BrainTransporter tech with Novartis' antibody.34

data.title: Key facts: Banco Santander completes 67.4% buyback; acquires TSB for $3.6B; Q3 profit down 0.3%
data.description:
• Banco Santander repurchased shares worth 1.15 billion Euros from November 6 to 12, 2025, completing 67.4% of its buyback program. Since 2021, it has bought back 15% of its shares.12
  • Banco Santander, led by Ana Botín, acquired UK lender TSB for $3.6 billion, removing a key M&A target for Barclays and confirming its UK unit is off the market.3
  • In Q3 2025, Banco Santander's Consumer Bank posted a pre-tax profit of 2,544 million NOK, down 0.3% year-over-year, with net interest income at 5,667 million NOK, a 1.6% decline.4

data.title: China Industrial Output Growth at 14-Month Low
data.description:
China’s industrial production grew by 4.9% year-on-year in October 2025, slowing from September’s three-month high of 6.5% and coming in below expectations of 5.5%.

This marked the softest increase in industrial production since August 2024.

data.title: Soybeans, wheat and corn set for weekly gains ahead of US crop report
data.description:
Chicago soybeans fell on Friday but remained on track for a weekly gain, as traders positioned themselves ahead of a U.S. government crop report and braced for the resumption of export data that may offer clues about Chinese buying.

Corn and wheat were also poised for weekly gains.

FUNDAMENTALS

* The most-active soybean contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) fell 0.17% to $11.45 a bushel, as of 0053 GMT, hovering near its highest level since June 2024.

* Corn dipped 0.11% to $4.41 a bushel. Wheat added 0.19% to $5.36-3/4 a bushel.

* Traders are awaiting long-anticipated U.S. Department of Agriculture supply/demand reports due Friday. The agency will also release a list of daily sales of U.S. agricultural products covering the last six weeks.

* Brazilian government supply agency Conab raised its estimate of the country's total 2025/26 corn production to 138.84 million metric tons, from 138.28 million in October.

* The agency also said Brazil will reap a record soybean crop of 177.6 million tons in the 2025/2026 harvest year.

* In Argentina, the Buenos Aires Grains Exchange raised its estimate of the country's wheat harvest to 24 million metric tons, and the Rosario Exchange on Wednesday raised its wheat forecast to a record 24.5 million tons.

* Commodity funds were net buyers of CBOT corn, soybean and wheat futures on Thursday, traders said. (COMFUND/CBT)

MARKET NEWS

* Asian shares joined a global selloff on Friday as hawkish comments from Federal Reserve officials doused hopes for a U.S. rate cut next month, while a still messy data calendar added to the angst, hitting bonds, the dollar and even gold.

data.title: China Retail Sales Growth Tops Estimates
data.description:
China’s retail sales rose 2.9% year-on-year in October 2025, easing slightly from September’s 3.0% increase and marking the weakest growth since August 2024.

Still, the result beat market expectations of 2.7%, supported by ongoing consumer subsidy programs. For the first ten months of 2025, retail sales were up 4.3% from a year earlier.

data.description:

TypeSell

SEC FilingForm 4

Joseph R. Young, SVP, Finance, and Chief Accounting Officer at Vera Therapeutics, and a 10% owner, sold 15,000 shares of Class A Common Stock on November 11, 2025, at a weighted-average price of $29.0583, totaling $435,874. data.title: China's property investment falls 14.7% y/y in January-October
data.description:
Property investment in China fell 14.7% in the first 10 months after a 13.9% drop in the first three quarters from the same period last year, official data showed on Friday.

Property sales by floor area dropped 6.8% year-on-year, compared with a 5.5% decline in the first nine months.

New construction starts measured by floor area declined 19.8% year-on-year, after diving 18.9% in January-September.

Funds raised by property developers in the first 10 months were down 9.7%, versus an 8.4% drop in the first three quarters.

data.title: JD Logistics: Appointed Zhenhui Wang as CEO, Executive Director After Wei Hu Resigned
Nidec Corp.'s Second-Quarter Net Profit Expected to More-Than Double — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Nidec Corp. is scheduled to report second-quarter results on Friday. Here's what you need to know:

NET PROFIT FORECAST: Nidec's net profit is estimated to have more than doubled to 47.6 billion yen, equivalent to $308 million, according to a poll of analysts by Visible Alpha. Net profit was Y19.39 billion in the year-earlier period.

REVENUE FORECAST: Second-quarter revenue is expected to have risen 1.1% to Y652.5 billion, according to the Visible Alpha poll.

The stock has fallen 21% year to date through Thursday, weighed by uncertainty over investigations into suspected accounting irregularities.

WHAT TO WATCH:

  • Nidec in October withdrew its earnings forecasts for the fiscal year ending March 2026 and said it wouldn't pay an interim dividend due to the ongoing investigations by a third-party committee. Investors will be focusing on any updates on the investigations and management's views on the business outlook.
  • First-quarter operating-profit margin declined to 9.6% from 9.3% a year earlier. Investors will be closely watching margin trends as businesses are dealing with relatively high inflation in many parts of the world.
  • Nidec has said that demand for water-cooling equipment used at data centers has grown. Investors will be paying attention to any initiatives by the company to capture artificial-intelligence-related demand.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: China Home Prices Continue Downward Spiral
data.description:
Home prices in major Chinese cities dropped at a faster pace in October from the previous month, official data showed Friday, suggesting continued weakness in the nation's real-estate market.

Average home prices across China's 70 biggest cities declined 0.45% in October, gaining pace from September's 0.41% drop, according to calculations by The Wall Street Journal based on data from the National Bureau of Statistics.

Out of the 70 cities, 64 reported month-on-month price declines, up from 63 in September.

Home prices fell 2.6% on year, compared with a 2.7% drop in September, with 61 cities posting year-over-year declines, the same as a month earlier.

Write to Singapore editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Thai baht/US dollar little changed on Friday
data.description:
The Thai baht was little changed against the U.S. dollar on Friday.

At 0143 GMT, the baht was at 32.33 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 32.310 to 32.375. It ended the previous session at 32.335 per dollar, as per LSEG data.
